repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body: ## Problem As of #39282, Nvim marks its start time, but there is no user-friendly way to get Nvim's uptime. ## Solution Add `:uptime` (based loosely on `uptime(1)`). ~~I haven't actually been able to fully test this, as I would need to leave Nvim open for two days to do a full test. But it should work :)~~ ### Demo

comment_data.body: i think our preferred convention is the other way
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -176,7 +177,7 @@ end
 --- Completion logic for `:lsp` command
 --- @param line string content of the current command line
 --- @return string[] list of completions
-function M.lsp_complete(line)
+M.lsp_complete = function(line)

comment_data.body:
i'd suggest starting a new `_core/time.lua` module, it's not unlikely we'll need that for other things later

that also will allow the tests to test the logic with a few more cases.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -256,4 +257,32 @@ M.ex_terminal = function(eap, shell_argv)
   end
 end
 
+--- @param seconds_in_unit integer How many seconds make up the unit.
+--- @param singular string The singular name of the unit. ("1 second")
+--- @param fplural string The plural name of the unit, to format. ("%s seconds")
+--- @param times string[] Working list of uptime strings.
+--- @param remaining integer Remaining time, in seconds.
+--- @return integer remaining Remaining time.
+local function time(seconds_in_unit, singular, fplural, times, remaining)

comment_data.body:
~~for testing purposes it should be fine to just make the first 2 args "dummy" args, and add a 3rd arg.~~

```suggestion
M.ex_uptime = function(notused1_, notused2_, remaining)
```
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -256,4 +257,32 @@ M.ex_terminal = function(eap, shell_argv)
   end
 end
 
+--- @param seconds_in_unit integer How many seconds make up the unit.
+--- @param singular string The singular name of the unit. ("1 second")
+--- @param fplural string The plural name of the unit, to format. ("%s seconds")
+--- @param times string[] Working list of uptime strings.
+--- @param remaining integer Remaining time, in seconds.
+--- @return integer remaining Remaining time.
+local function time(seconds_in_unit, singular, fplural, times, remaining)
+  local unit = math.floor(remaining / seconds_in_unit)
+  if unit ~= 0 or #times ~= 0 or seconds_in_unit == 1 then
+    local display = unit == 1 and singular or fplural:format(unit)
+    times[#times + 1] = display
+  end
+  return remaining % seconds_in_unit
+end
+
+M.ex_uptime = function()

comment_data.body: this can stay here, but the other logic should probably be extracted (probably to `_core/time.lua`) to return something, so that tests can easily assert it.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -256,4 +257,32 @@ M.ex_terminal = function(eap, shell_argv)
   end
 end
 
+--- @param seconds_in_unit integer How many seconds make up the unit.
+--- @param singular string The singular name of the unit. ("1 second")
+--- @param fplural string The plural name of the unit, to format. ("%s seconds")
+--- @param times string[] Working list of uptime strings.
+--- @param remaining integer Remaining time, in seconds.
+--- @return integer remaining Remaining time.
+local function time(seconds_in_unit, singular, fplural, times, remaining)
+  local unit = math.floor(remaining / seconds_in_unit)
+  if unit ~= 0 or #times ~= 0 or seconds_in_unit == 1 then
+    local display = unit == 1 and singular or fplural:format(unit)
+    times[#times + 1] = display
+  end
+  return remaining % seconds_in_unit
+end
+
+M.ex_uptime = function()
+  local remaining = math.floor((uv.hrtime() - vim.v.starttime) / 1e9)
+
+  local times = {}
+  remaining = time(86400, N_('1 day'), N_('%s days'), times, remaining)
+  remaining = time(3600, N_('1 hour'), N_('%s hours'), times, remaining)
+  remaining = time(60, N_('1 minute'), N_('%s minutes'), times, remaining)
+  remaining = time(1, N_('1 second'), N_('%s seconds'), times, remaining)
+  assert(remaining == 0)
+
+  api.nvim_echo({ { N_('Up %s'):format(table.concat(times, ', ')) } }, true, {})

@@ -0,0 +1,35 @@
+local N_ = vim.fn.gettext
+
+local M = {}
+
+--- @param seconds_in_unit integer How many seconds make up the unit.
+--- @param singular string The singular name of the unit. ("1 second")
+--- @param fplural string The plural name of the unit, to format. ("%s seconds")
+--- @param times string[] Working list of uptime strings.
+--- @param remaining integer Remaining time, in seconds.
+--- @return integer remaining Remaining time.
+local function time_part(seconds_in_unit, singular, fplural, times, remaining)
+  local unit = math.floor(remaining / seconds_in_unit)
+  if unit ~= 0 or #times ~= 0 or seconds_in_unit == 1 then
+    local display = unit == 1 and singular or fplural:format(unit)
+    times[#times + 1] = display
+  end
+  return remaining % seconds_in_unit
+end
+
+--- Display seconds in a pretty form (e.g. "1 hour, 24 minutes, 13 seconds").
+---
+--- @param seconds integer Time in seconds.
+--- @return string time Pretty representation of the time.
+function M.pretty_rtime(seconds)

comment_data.body:
WDYT about making it a bit more "DWIM", so e.g. if it's >2 days, it shows `X days (started: 20260101.xx)` (smaller units are not that useful), etc? Similar to how the `uptime` shell command behaves:

```
$ uptime
21:16  up 21 days,  4:22,
```



It's not something we need to solve right now. But maybe days or months from now :)
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -0,0 +1,35 @@
+local N_ = vim.fn.gettext
+
+local M = {}
+
+--- @param seconds_in_unit integer How many seconds make up the unit.
+--- @param singular string The singular name of the unit. ("1 second")
+--- @param fplural string The plural name of the unit, to format. ("%s seconds")
+--- @param times string[] Working list of uptime strings.
+--- @param remaining integer Remaining time, in seconds.
+--- @return integer remaining Remaining time.
+local function time_part(seconds_in_unit, singular, fplural, times, remaining)
+  local unit = math.floor(remaining / seconds_in_unit)
+  if unit ~= 0 or #times ~= 0 or seconds_in_unit == 1 then
+    local display = unit == 1 and singular or fplural:format(unit)
+    times[#times + 1] = display
+  end
+  return remaining % seconds_in_unit
+end
+
+--- Display seconds in a pretty form (e.g. "1 hour, 24 minutes, 13 seconds").

comment_data.body: not sure we want to perpetuate this C convention. but maybe, idk.

I based it on `uptime -p`. It's definitely an compelling idea to provide information like this, though. I agree most people won't care about seconds when Nvim has been open for days :)

Unfortunately we need a datetime lua API to show a starting date/time like you've suggested.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -0,0 +1,35 @@

+--- @return integer remaining Remaining time.

+  if unit ~= 0 or #times ~= 0 or seconds_in_unit == 1 then

+    times[#times + 1] = display
